provenance.source_corpus: Carbon and Nitrogen Mineralization and Microbial Succession in Oil Sands Reclamation Soils Amended with Pyrogenic Carbon

source_spans.text: PyC amendments would increase C mineralization and decrease N mineralization in all soils
Results indicated that the different PyC types increased C mineralization compared to the control
In contrast, PyC caused a decrease in N mineralization
Both diversity indices indicate an increase in the overall diversity of the bacterial communities within the treated groups when compared to the non-treated control
Petroleum coke addition to soil lowers levels of C mineralization
fungal community diversity from FFM which drops in response to biochar
fungal community diversity from FFM which drops in response to biochar
